    This week’s outside five sigma (#29)

    January 12, 2025 6 minute read

    Wentao

    Memories are the long term averages, and the outside five sigmas.

    • Twitter
    • GitHub
    • Google Scholar

    On this page

    • High-Power Miniature Laser (PCSEL)
    • Macrscopic Carbon Nanotube
    • FBG Sensors for Battery Monitoring
    • Ti flexure Lens Alignment for Space
    • IR Microlens Arrays
    • Heat Engines and Information Theory

    • Fractal Capacitor Designs

    • IBM Electrostatic Clutch Development

    • Detailed Optical Resonator Protocols
    • Real-Time Neutrino (muon) Detection Monitoring
    • Japanese Neutrinos Reach Korea
    • Compact Superconducting Capacitor Designs

    High-Power Miniature Laser (PCSEL)

    Noda group’s 3 mm diameter, 50 W continuous-wave single-mode photonic-crystal surface-emitting laser (PCSEL).

    • Look at all the ribbon bonds to inject >100 A current into this little chip, liquid cooled with 6°C water.
    • No other semiconductor lasers could rival PCSEL’s power density, linewidth (3 pm), and beam quality & divergence (0.05 deg), that are approaching gas, solid state, or fiber lasers.
      • Image from The Tiny Ultrabright Laser that Can Melt Steel
      • specs from Yoshida2023: High-brightness scalable continuous-wave single-mode photonic-crystal laser
      • It’s >10 yr of work from them to improve the single mode lasing condition, peak power, and finally CW operation.
      • Morita2021
      • Yoshida2018
      • Hirose2014
      • Early work done by a German group: Riechel2000
      • As well as recent similar work from Berkeley (they call it BerkSEL):
        • Kanté2024
        • Contractor2022
      • Also there are grain of rice size lasers with 50A and 50 W CW

    Macrscopic Carbon Nanotube

    Folks wrap your perforated hex metal sheet into a tube, you could also become a carbon nanotube researcher (In their case it’s a giant stainless steel cylinder with pillars from precision cnc milling..)

    • Zheng2024: Surface-phononic carbon nanotubes

    FBG Sensors for Battery Monitoring

    A nice review on using fiber Bragg grating (FBG) for temperature and strain-stress sensing in batteries.

    • Liu2024: Advanced Functional Optical Fiber Sensors for Smart Battery Monitoring

    Ti flexure Lens Alignment for Space

    A titanium flexure mount for lateral alignment between a lens and an array of SNSPDs.

    • Wollman2024: SNSPD-based detector system for NASA’s Deep Space Optical Communications project

    IR Microlens Arrays

    AR coated silicon microlens array for 25 um light. Look at the kilopixel one!

    • Left: Day2024: A 25-micrometer Single-Photon-Sensitive Kinetic Inductance Detector
    • Right: Cothard2023: Monolithic Kilopixel Silicon Microlens Arrays for Future Far-Infrared Observatories
    • People used to have fun and do reflow for the microlens. Now it’s all spoiled kids doing grayscale lithography. (grayscale is cool)
    • Popovic1988: Technique for monolithic fabrication of microlens arrays

    Heat Engines and Information Theory

    Ok heat engines are information arbitrageurs, but when will we get information arbitrageurs as heat engines?

    • Leighton2024: Information Arbitrage in Bipartite Heat Engines

    Broadband Rb Microwave Spectrum Analyzer

    Another nice example of converting between time/frequency domain and spatial domain to achieve large bandwidth: A Rb vapor cell sits next to a magnet, so the Zeeman shift of the hyperfine levels are ~linear wrt. distance to the magnet, which changes the microwave frequency it gets excited at. A laser pulse pumps the atoms to the ground state, followed by the microwave excitation, and probe & imaging. Although the power detection limit is high, they achieved 1 GHz (>25 GHz expected after further optimization) instantaneous BW with 3 MHz resolution around 13 GHz. Typical real-time spectrum analyzers give you few hundred MHz to few GHz.

    • Shi2024: Broad Instantaneous Bandwidth Microwave Spectrum Analyzer with a Microfabricated Atomic Vapor Cell

    Fast Optical Switching with PT symmetry

    Interesting optical switch with “PT-symmetry”: use gain to adjust the coupling rate so that it is optically controlled, and much much faster than MEMS alternative (100 ps vs 0.1~1 us). I’m usually a PT symmetry hater, but I like this!

    • Now only if they could get the off state IL to be ~< 0.1 dB…
      • Feng2025: Non-Hermitian hybrid silicon photonic switching
      • The MEMS version from Ming Wu’s group, very sick structures. The array is so big it is stitching multiple reticles. The IL and extinction are also much better.
      • And since they are using pull-in effect, they only need 2n control lines for n^2 switches.
      • Seok2019: Wafer-scale silicon photonic switches beyond die size limit

    Interactive 3D Model Demonstrations

    Does your model really bear the weight of your code name? (they have cool interactive 3d scenes on the demo website)

    • project-prometheus

    Laser Pointer Regulations in Switzerland

    TIL all laser pointers above class 1 are banned in Switzerland, from owning to distributing. US: don’t promote lasers above class 3B as “laser pointers”.

    • Switzerland federal office of public health FOPH - Laser pointers

    Calculator Models in Research Methods

    First time seeing a calculator model described in the method

    • Schreck2025: Wafer bow in diamond heteroepitaxy: Causes, their analytical description, and viable solutions

    Bent Waveguides for Broadband Coupling

    hmm directional coupler (DC) but bent to achieve a larger bandwidth. The group velocity difference between the even and odd supermodes does not reach zero in a straight DC -> the coupling depends on wavelength to first order. It became possible with bent waveguide -> coupling can be tuned to be first order independent of wavelength. (still not really broadband)

    • Saeed2024: Low-Loss Silicon Directional Coupler with Arbitrary Coupling Ratios for Broadband Wavelength Operation Based on Bent Waveguides
    • The idea is old as in early 2000s.
    • Doerr2005: Bending of a Planar Lightwave Circuit 2 2 Coupler to Desensitize it to Wavelength, Polarization, and Fabrication Changes

    Fractal Capacitor Designs

    Another freaking weird capacitor

    • Anferov2024: Millimeter-wave Superconducting Quantum Devices

    Poetic PhD Thesis Acknowledgments

    The unexpected poetry of PhD acknowledgements, from ANU College of Science and Medicine Communications Team.

    • Not just the acknowledgements, look at these thesis titles.
      • The unexpected poetry of PhD acknowledgements

    IBM Electrostatic Clutch Development

    When IBM was still working on mechanical machines (electrostatic clutch)

    • Fitch1957: Development of the Electrostatic Clutch
    • The newer ones from Carnegie Mellon
    • Diller2016: A lightweight, low-power electroadhesive clutch and spring for exoskeleton actuation
    • They have a company

    Early OCR with Minimal Computing

    People used to do OCR with ~100 “flops” of compute, and ~10 kB/s “memory bandwidth”, what’s your excuse? (an IBM 650. It did cost “$150,000 in 1959, or roughly $1,500,000 as of 2023”)

    • Greanias1957: Design of Logic for Recognition of Printed Characters by Simulation

    Detailed Optical Resonator Protocols

    TIL theres a nature protocol journal and it’s actually good: this paper on using optical resonator for biochem sensing has details about all aspects of it including math, fab, surface prep, optical coupling, packaging etc., with actual SOPs and part numbers etc.

    • How detailed it is? It has the best descriptions on what and how to make a tapered fiber than anywhere else I’ve seen, better than papers that are just about tapered fibers. (Their example transmission of it sucks tho you should be able to get >90%)
    • This honestly has changed my view a bit on this fashion magazine series called nature.
    • Suebka2025: Frequency locked whispering evanescent resonator (FLOWER) for biochemical sensing applications
      • heres mine in poor quality from a webcam

    Real-Time Neutrino (muon) Detection Monitoring

    TIL the Super-Kamiokande has a realtime monitor that shows you the map of signals from the ~13,000 photomultiplier tubes. It updates every few seconds, as cosmic ray muon events are ~2 Hz. Here is a fresh screenshot I just took.

    • Because I saw this vid this morning
    • Hamamatsu has an article about the tv-sized PMT
    • They are building a newer one that holds 5x more water and uses ~4x more PMTs

    Japanese Neutrinos Reach Korea

    Hello my Korean friends, would you like some Japanese neutrinos today?

    • Lodovico2017: The Hyper-Kamiokande Experiment

    Compact Superconducting Capacitor Designs

    Another crazy capacitor, few hundred um diameter parallel plate with down to 75 nm vacuum gap. The paper included many process details, stress tuning, failure modes at different steps, actual pictures of the wafers and the packaging, and example layout, very nice.

    • Saw this process in 2022, and they finally wrote it up after a few big papers. Youssefi2025: Compact superconducting vacuum-gap capacitors with low microwave loss and high mechanical coherence for scalable quantum circuits
    • See also this tweet
    • Damn they changed all the names and affiliations on GRC website to be pngs..

    Tags: Capacitor, Flexure, Lens, MEMS, Microwave, Nanotube, Neutrino, OCR, Optical_switch, Optics, PCSEL, Photonics, RSA, Taper, WGM
